content.full_text:
FDPC TRANSMITTAL OF CORRESPONDENCE

CORRESPONDENT Thomas Kevies Broadus                DATE
                                                    REC'D. 3/2/81

DATED 2/26/81  RE: campaign spending

ADMINISTRATION          CONFLICTS/LEGAL        ENFORCEMENT
___BOB A.              ___TOM S.              ___ROB B.
___LINDA              ___STELLA              ___ROGER
___BEV
                      ___BARBARA
EXECUTIVE             ___DIANE               TA&A
___TOM H.             ___PATSY               ___LYNN
___TED                                       ___CARLA
___NANCY W.           A-81-02-011            ___MARYANN    ___JAY
___JEANNE                                    ___BERMA      ___RICH
___BOB S.                                                  ___HELEN-L.A
___JOHN


___INFORMATION Rich___REQUEST FOR ADVICE--MUST BE ANSWERED WITHIN 21 D

___Jay___PLEASE:

___TAKE WHATEVER ACTION YOU CONSIDER APPROPRIATE
___REPLY DIRECT BY _____________ -- COPY TO
___PREPARE SUGGESTED REPLY AND RETURN TO Oliver____
___COORDINATE REPLY WITH _______________


3/1/81    Called M. Keener -

1) He committed should fall on 420, 450 & 490
   depending on nature of contribution; may quote things
   attached pt 4 of handbook & a Remnant

2) If committee had no activity during 1979-1980
   no comparements will be filed

3) Sent M. Keener the appropriate forms & a
   campaign disclosure filled him to all
   med he had further questions
                                        JL E.

THOMAS KEISER
735 West Duarte Road
Suite #407
Arcadia, California 91006

February 26, 1981

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street Building
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Friends of Tom Keiser Committee #781391

Dear Sirs:

The above-referenced committee has had no activity
since its formation in 1978. The committee was formed to
hold approximately $600.00 left over from a City Council
Campaign. I now want to use the committee to hold, in May,
a fundraiser for a future campaign. Please tell me what,
if any forms, I will be required to file.

Since 1978 I have filed, as an officeholder, a short
form declaration of no activity. I have assumed that also
covered my committee. Is that correct? If not, what forms
do I need to file at this time?

Thank you for your anticipated assistance in this
matter. If you have any questions, please call me at (213)
681-0401.

Very truly yours,

THOMAS KEISER

TK:kt

embedding.qa_text: This document is an FPPC (Fair Political Practices Commission) transmittal of correspondence dated 2/26/81, addressed to Thomas Keiser, Arcadia. The RE: subject line indicates "campaign reporting." The document lists several names under the categories of Administration, Executive, Conflicts/Legal, and Enforcement, with some names checked off as JAY, RICH, HELEN-L.A., and TA&A. The information section states that this is a request for advice, which must be answered within 21 days. It instructs to take whatever action you consider appropriate, reply direct by copy to, prepare suggested reply and return to, and coordinate reply with. Handwritten notes at the bottom of the page indicate: 3/9/81 Called Mr. Keiser - 1) The committee should fill on 420, 450 or 490 depending on nature of contributions; may require filing of threshold statement for fundraisers or a permanent annual. 2) If committee had no activity during 1979 & 1980, no campaign statements need be filed. 3) Sent Mr. Keiser the appropriate forms and a Campaign Disclosure Manual and told him to call me if he had further questions. J.R.E. **THOMAS KEISER 735 West Duarte Road Suite #407 Arcadia, California 91006** February 26, 1981 Fain Political Practices Commission 1100 "K" Street Building Sacramento, California 95814 Re: Friends of Tom Keiser Committee #781391 Dear Sirs: The above-referenced committee has had no activity since its formation in 1978. The committee was formed to hold approximately $600.00 left over from a City Council Campaign. I now want to use the committee to hold, in May, a fundraiser for a future campaign. Please tell me what, if any forms, I will be required to file. Since 1978 I have filed, as an officeholder, a short form declaration of no activity. I have assumed that also covered my committee. Is that correct? If not, what forms do I need to file at this time? Thank you for your anticipated assistance in this matter. If you have any questions, please call me at (213) 681-0401. Very truly yours, [signature] THOMAS KEISER TK:kt
